evidence: `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` is a 226-importer hub (packet holistic_context) and directly imports UI toast infrastructure, so non-UI callers depend on UI-layer wiring.

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` still runs runtime setup from module evaluation (`export const supabase = getOrCreateSupabaseClient()`), including instrumentation/global registration/initialization, while the same file documents avoiding import-time side effects.

`src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx` is a broad shared dependency (66 importers in packet) combining UA parsing, pointer heuristics, orientation, and multiple hooks, making behavior changes ripple across unrelated feature areas.

`src/pages/Home/components/InstallInstructionsModal.tsx` (441 LOC) bundles many platform/browser-specific mock subcomponents in one file, mixing presentation variants and orchestration responsibility.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/LightboxShell.tsx` (437 LOC) combines modal shell ownership with extensive input-event policy, z-index conflict policy, scroll-lock policy, and layout adaptation in a single unit.

`src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/components/ShotActions.tsx` (262 LOC) aggregates UI rendering plus shot navigation/addition workflow decisions through multiple state bags (`selector`, `status`, `quickCreate`, `actions`), increasing ownership blur.

Task payload shaping is repeated with near-identical legacy-to-unified structure guidance conversion paths in `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts` (structure_guidance branches + legacy cleanup at lines ~182-296) and `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts` (LEGACY_STRUCTURE_PARAMS + cleanup at lines ~140-156 and ~479-485).

Multiple task modules maintain large, overlapping snake_case parameter bags and ad-hoc field passthroughs (`src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts` lines ~276-399, `src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts` lines ~141-203, `src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts` lines ~408-423), increasing abstraction surface without a shared contract layer.

Lightbox model assembly builds very wide prop objects that are mostly pass-through plumbing into downstream hooks/components in both image and video flows (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx` lines ~548-685, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` lines ~172-334).

Image and video lightbox flows duplicate boundary choreography (build state value -> derive handlers -> assemble workflow props -> render layout) with different local names but similar seam logic in `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx` and `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx`.

Both lightbox paths manually compose large `useMemo` dependency lists and handoff bundles into panel/layout components (`ImageLightbox.tsx` lines ~417-523 and ~632-685; `useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` lines ~39-143 and ~291-334), making integration seams fragile to small shape changes.

Task orchestration seams rely on repeated, module-local mapping and cleanup conventions instead of one translation boundary (`src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`), so behavioral fixes require synchronized multi-file edits.

Edge-function internals use broad `unknown`/unchecked object access rather than narrow typed decoding in core control flow (`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts`: `taskData: unknown` with direct property reads like `taskData.params`, `taskData.category`, `taskData.task_type`).

Type safety is bypassed via force-casting query results (`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts` line ~227 casts `task` to `TaskWithProject`), then deep fields are read throughout the function, coupling logic to runtime shape assumptions.

Local helper signatures advertise minimal types while implementations still depend on dynamic nested records (`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts` uses `Record<string, unknown>` for params and repeatedly accesses nested orchestrator fields), reducing precision at branch points.

Batch-drop coordinate naming is inconsistent across related APIs: `onBatchFileDrop`/`onBatchGenerationDrop` are documented as `targetFrame` in `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/ShotSettingsContext.tsx:83` and `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/ShotSettingsContext.tsx:85`, but typed as `targetPosition` in `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/types.ts:104` and `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/types.ts:106`.

Invalidation API completion semantics are inconsistent: `invalidateGenerationsSync` can defer work with `setTimeout` despite its sync name in `src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts:84`, while adjacent orchestration helpers expose explicit async completion (`runSerializedTimelineWrite` returns completion promise in `src/shared/lib/timelineWriteQueue.ts:108`, and `useTaskPlaceholder` awaits refetches before cleanup in `src/shared/hooks/useTaskPlaceholder.ts:94`).

Edge utility APIs report success-like outcomes even on degraded paths: `getStoragePublicUrl` returns `{ exists: true }` without existence verification in `supabase/functions/complete_task/storage.ts:177`, and `checkRateLimit` returns `allowed: true` on RPC failure in `supabase/functions/_shared/rateLimit.ts:79`.
`src/shared/hooks/usePromptFieldState.ts` has a long tutorial-style header and in-file usage example (lines 1-15, 85-109) that repeats implementation details rather than documenting constraints.

`src/shared/hooks/useServerForm.ts` includes extensive narrative comments and embedded sample usage (lines 1-36) plus many restating comments through core logic (e.g., lines 90, 130, 136, 153, 171, 182, 203), increasing code-to-signal ratio.

`src/shared/lib/debug/debugRendering.ts` and `src/shared/lib/debug/debugPolling.ts` both use verbose explanatory comment blocks around straightforward behavior, which adds maintenance overhead for basic utilities.

`src/shared/hooks/shots/index.ts` explicitly preserves a backwards-compatibility import path while `src/shared/hooks/useShots.ts` re-exports that barrel as canonical, leaving dual entry points active (useShots.ts line 8; shots/index.ts lines 3-6).

`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/types.ts` keeps multiple deprecated fields inside the main `ProjectImageSettings` interface (lines 98-118), so new and legacy settings shapes coexist in the same active contract.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts` retains many deprecated task params and a compatibility shim path (lines 5-35), indicating migration is still open-ended rather than bounded by a removal plan.

Packet structure shows `src/shared/hooks/` is a flat directory with 97 production files and mixed concerns (AI, preloading, auth/API keys, timeline, shots, segments), exceeding the >10 mixed-concern split threshold.

Packet structure shows `src/shared/components/` has 80 files with mixed zones (`production`: 60, `test`: 20); assigned files include both production and tests at the same level (`src/shared/components/ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ActiveLoRAsDisplay.test.tsx`, `src/shared/components/AppErrorBoundary.test.tsx`).

Coupling signals show heavy cross-directory edges (`src/shared/components/ → src/shared/hooks/` = 59, `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/ → src/shared/hooks/` = 99), reinforcing that broad top-level buckets are carrying too many unrelated responsibilities.

Timeline behavior and invariants are split across frontend math/drag utilities (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/utils/timeline-utils.ts`) and persistence orchestration (`src/shared/hooks/timeline/useTimelineFrameUpdates.ts`), while DB-side timeline semantics were repeatedly redefined across many migrations (for example `supabase/migrations/20250922140100_create_timeline_sync_bulletproof.sql`, `supabase/migrations/20250922230843_fix_timeline_sync_bulletproof.sql`, `supabase/migrations/20250923009000_final_timeline_fix.sql`).

Shot mutation workflows are implemented in parallel hooks with overlapping but non-identical behavior (`src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/hooks/useShotActions.ts`, `src/shared/components/TasksPane/hooks/useShotActions.ts`, `src/pages/shots/hooks/useShotImageMutations.ts`), indicating feature-by-feature duplication instead of a single shot-action service boundary.

Lightbox architecture remains highly compositional but still carries broad responsibility surfaces through prop bundling and orchestrator hooks (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/MediaLightbox.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useSharedLightboxState.ts`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useImageEditOrchestrator.ts`), which increases indirection and makes behavior ownership hard to trace.

Project-scoped cache hooks are near-structural clones with slight API divergence (`src/shared/hooks/useProjectGenerationModesCache.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useProjectVideoCountsCache.ts`), signaling missing shared abstraction for cache lifecycle + query synchronization.

Style island in UI primitives uses double quotes/no semicolons (`src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx:1`, `src/shared/components/ui/label.tsx:1`, `src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx:1`, `src/shared/components/ui/sonner.tsx:1`) while many other assigned files use single quotes + semicolons.
Intra-file convention drift in `src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx` mixes quote/semicolon styles in the same module (e.g., lines 1-5 vs 10-18 and 77-90).
Naming/comment style differs by area (UI wrappers use terse shadcn-style declarations; task/hooks modules use more explicit typed prose), creating inconsistent local expectations for sibling files.

Centralized `handleError` contract exists (`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts:24`), but several assigned files still use direct `console.error` with ad-hoc strings (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts:57`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts:507`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts:73`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts:138`).

Silent catch patterns are used in multiple places (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/StrokeOverlay.tsx:166`, `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/promptManagement/utils.ts:11`, `src/pages/Home/hooks/useDiscordSignIn.ts:10`) with no shared policy for when swallow/log/report is acceptable.

Failure semantics vary for similar boundary operations: throw+rethrow in task creation (`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts:433-436`, `514-520`) vs return-null on failure in orchestrator queries (`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts:215`) vs user-toast-and-return patterns (`src/shared/components/LoraSelectorModal/components/MyLorasTab/hooks/useLoraSubmission.ts:364-387`).

`calculate-task-cost` explicitly enforces service-role auth via `authenticateRequest` and `auth.isServiceRole` (`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts:186-196`), but sibling completion logic files perform privileged task/orchestrator updates without local auth guard in-file (`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts:34-41`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts:39-46`).

Client-side Supabase instance is exported to global window symbols (`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts:9-10`), widening practical access surface for any in-page script despite other modules assuming controlled access via imports.

Route auth gating uses cached localStorage token presence (`src/app/routes.tsx:39-55`) as a fast pre-check while other areas depend on authoritative runtime/session checks, indicating mixed trust-boundary patterns.

findings.summary: Core error handler is coupled to UI toast layer, widening blast radius.
evidence: `handleError` imports `@/shared/components/ui/toast` directly and is used by many non-UI hooks/handlers.
Callers like media hooks invoke `handleError` for control-flow error reporting, so UI notification policy is transitively embedded in shared logic.
findings.suggestion: Split error processing into a UI-agnostic core (`normalize/log/return`) and an app-boundary notifier adapter. Inject notification behavior via options or a boundary-level wrapper so shared modules do not import UI components.

findings.summary: Supabase client module still triggers runtime init on import.
evidence: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` exports `supabase = getOrCreateSupabaseClient()` at module scope, which executes instrumentation/global registration/init when imported.
The same file comments state intent to avoid import-time side effects, creating architecture contract drift.
Packet context marks this file as a high fan-in hub (146 importers), so boot-time coupling affects a large portion of the app.
findings.suggestion: Export a pure accessor (`getSupabaseClient`) and move initialization into explicit app bootstrap or feature entrypoints. Keep module evaluation side-effect free and gate runtime setup behind explicit calls.

findings.summary: Mobile/tablet detection is centralized as a broad policy hub.
evidence: `useMobile.tsx` mixes UA regex, pointer checks, orientation, tablet/phone inference, and aggregate device API in one shared module.
Feature components/hooks consume this shared policy directly (`CreativePartnerPane`, mobile gallery interactions), coupling feature behavior to one heuristic source.
Packet context reports high reuse (66 importers), so changing heuristics has broad cross-feature impact.
findings.suggestion: Decompose device policy into narrower modules (`viewport`, `input-capability`, `ua-fallback`) and expose a small stable interface per use-case, reducing global coupling to one monolithic heuristic file.

findings.summary: Several UI files combine orchestration and variant-heavy implementation.
evidence: `InstallInstructionsModal` keeps many browser/platform mock components plus modal orchestration in one file.
`LightboxShell` owns shell rendering and multiple independent policy domains (pointer/click routing, touch filtering, scroll lock, z-index arbitration).
`ShotActions` coordinates shot selection, navigation, quick-create, and position/no-position add flows while also rendering full control UI.
findings.suggestion: Refactor by ownership boundary: extract policy/state machines into dedicated hooks/services and keep components focused on composition. For each file, move variant subcomponents or action policies into adjacent modules with narrow interfaces.

findings.summary: Legacy-to-unified payload translation is duplicated across task builders.
evidence: `payloadBuilder.ts` and `individualTravelSegment.ts` both implement structure-guidance normalization and legacy key stripping independently.
Each module maintains its own list of legacy keys and transformation rules, so migration fixes must be repeated manually.
`joinClips.ts` adds another custom orchestrator-detail assembler with similar optional passthrough and override behavior.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared task-payload normalization module (e.g., `shared/lib/tasks/orchestratorPayload.ts`) that owns structure-guidance normalization, legacy key cleanup, and optional-field compaction; have all task builders call it instead of duplicating conversion logic.

findings.summary: Image/video lightbox seams duplicate orchestration and prop plumbing.
evidence: Both flows build similarly shaped layout/action/buttonGroup bundles and pass them into `useLightboxWorkflowProps` separately.
Both flows implement near-identical selector-navigation and panel-task-id fallback logic.
Both flows carry very large dependency arrays and intermediate wrappers, increasing breakage risk when shared contracts evolve.
findings.suggestion: Create a shared lightbox seam adapter (`buildLightboxRenderContract`) that centralizes common workflow prop assembly and shared handlers; keep only media-specific branches in image/video modules.

findings.summary: Core edge flows depend on unchecked runtime shapes and casts.
evidence: `createGenerationFromTask` accepts `taskData: unknown` but immediately reads nested fields without decoding.
`calculate-task-cost` force-casts query output to `TaskWithProject` and then assumes joined relations exist.
Orchestrator/generation logic repeatedly traverses nested `Record<string, unknown>` params at branch-critical decision points.
findings.suggestion: Introduce explicit runtime decoders (zod or narrow custom guards) for task payload/query shapes at function boundaries, then operate on validated typed objects in routing/cost logic.

findings.summary: Batch drop APIs mix `targetFrame` and `targetPosition` for the same interaction.
evidence: `ShotSettingsContext` documents batch handlers as frame-based (`uses targetFrame`) while the editor action types expose `targetPosition` parameters.
This requires adapters to reinterpret coordinates between modules and makes caller expectations unstable.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one coordinate contract (`targetFrame` recommended to match timeline semantics), rename the batch callbacks consistently, and add a narrow adapter type only at legacy boundaries until all call sites are migrated.

findings.summary: Invalidation APIs expose mixed completion guarantees with misleading naming.
evidence: `invalidateGenerationsSync` can schedule delayed async work via `setTimeout`, so callers cannot rely on completion despite the `Sync` suffix.
Neighboring helpers (`runSerializedTimelineWrite`, `useTaskPlaceholder`) expose explicit completion points and are awaited in orchestration paths.
findings.suggestion: Split invalidation into two explicit APIs: `scheduleGenerationInvalidation` (fire-and-forget) and `invalidateGenerationNow` (awaitable promise), then remove `Sync` naming and update callers to choose the intended contract.

findings.summary: Multiple seam helpers hide failures behind normal-looking return shapes.
evidence: Rate-limit failures return `allowed: true` (fail-open), which is operationally valid but indistinguishable from a healthy limiter without extra fields.
`getStoragePublicUrl` marks `exists: true` when a URL is constructed, not when object existence is checked.
Clip metadata and pending clip consumers downgrade errors to `durationSeconds: 0` / `[]` without a typed degraded-status channel.
findings.suggestion: Introduce explicit degraded-state fields in boundary return types (for example `sourceStatus: 'ok'|'degraded'|'failed'` with reason codes), keep current fallbacks, and require downstream orchestration to branch on the status before assuming normal behavior.

findings.summary: Shot hooks migration keeps old and new import surfaces active in parallel
evidence: `useShots.ts` exports `*` from `./shots` as canonical API.
`shots/index.ts` states it is kept for backwards compatibility and legacy top-level imports, so both paths remain first-class.
findings.suggestion: Choose one stable public path (prefer `src/shared/hooks/useShots.ts`), codemod internal imports to it, then deprecate and remove the legacy barrel in `src/shared/hooks/shots/index.ts` behind a dated migration ticket.

findings.summary: Deprecated migration fields are still embedded in active settings/task interfaces
evidence: `ProjectImageSettings` includes many `@deprecated` properties alongside current fields, keeping write/read ambiguity in one interface.
`LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParams` keeps old structure-video keys and explicit shim commentary, but no bounded removal marker.
findings.suggestion: Split legacy input shapes into dedicated `Legacy*` adapter types and conversion functions at ingress boundaries, keep runtime-normalized modern types in core interfaces, and add a removal deadline comment/ticket ID for each remaining legacy key.

findings.summary: Several hooks/utils contain tutorial-style commentary that outweighs executable signal
evidence: `usePromptFieldState.ts` duplicates priority logic in large prose blocks plus inline comments and sample usage.
`useServerForm.ts` includes extensive narrative and repeated intent comments around nearly every block.
`debugRendering.ts` documents basic dev-only logger behavior with long explanatory sections that restate code.
findings.suggestion: Trim in-file comments to invariants and non-obvious decisions only, move examples/usage walkthroughs to docs, and keep each exported hook with a short contract-focused header (inputs, outputs, side effects).

findings.summary: Core helpers rely on broad casts and synthetic fallback objects instead of precise narrowing
evidence: `extractSegmentImages` repeatedly casts unknown params to specific shapes without runtime guards, then indexes arrays assumed to exist.
`useServerForm` uses `({} as TLocal)` when data is absent, which fabricates structurally valid-but-empty local data.
findings.suggestion: Introduce small runtime shape guards (`isRecord`, `isStringArray`) in `galleryUtils` and replace `({} as TLocal)` in `useServerForm` with an explicit `emptyLocal` initializer input or `null`-aware API contract so invalid intermediate states are not masked.

findings.summary: Top-level `src/shared/components` mixes broad production UI and tests, obscuring package boundaries.
evidence: `src/shared/components/` profile has 80 files with mixed zones (`production`: 60, `test`: 20), indicating flat overload.
Assigned test files are in the same top-level folder as production files instead of a focused test boundary.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) create feature folders and test boundaries (`src/shared/components/lora/`, `src/shared/components/error-boundaries/`, matching `__tests__/` folders). 2) move `ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx` to `src/shared/components/lora/ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx`; move `ActiveLoRAsDisplay.test.tsx` and `AppErrorBoundary.test.tsx` into the corresponding `__tests__` folders. 3) update imports with `desloppify move src/shared/components/ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx src/shared/components/lora/ActiveLoRAsDisplay.tsx --dry-run` then run without `--dry-run`; repeat for tests. 4) validate with `pnpm vitest src/shared/components/lora/__tests__/ActiveLoRAsDisplay.test.tsx src/shared/components/error-boundaries/__tests__/AppErrorBoundary.test.tsx` and `pnpm vitest`.

findings.summary: Distinct domain hooks are co-located in one large shared package, weakening ownership and layering clarity.
evidence: `src/shared/hooks/` has 97 production files and imports heavily from infra (`src/integrations/supabase/`: 49, `src/shared/lib/`: 62), indicating broad, mixed ownership.
The three assigned hooks belong to different capability slices (AI interaction, gallery preloading, API key persistence) but share one package namespace and one test bucket.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) define capability folders under hooks (`src/shared/hooks/ai/`, `src/shared/hooks/gallery/`, `src/shared/hooks/integrations/`) with mirrored test folders. 2) move `useAIInteractionService` + test first, then `useAdjacentPagePreloader` + test, then `useApiKeys` + test to minimize merge risk. 3) use import-safe moves (`desloppify move src/shared/hooks/useAIInteractionService.ts src/shared/hooks/ai/useAIInteractionService.ts --dry-run`, then apply; repeat for each file and matching tests). 4) validate with `pnpm vitest src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAIInteractionService.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useAdjacentPagePreloader.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useApiKeys.test.ts` and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: Core error contract is coupled to UI toast side effects across 200+ importers.
evidence: `handleError` imports `@/shared/components/ui/toast` and conditionally triggers UI toast inside a shared-lib primitive.
Holistic context flags `handleError.ts` as a god module with 226 importers, so UI coupling leaks into non-UI modules.
README positions architecture docs as contract source, but this cross-layer side-effect boundary is not surfaced there.
findings.suggestion: Split error core from presentation adapter: keep `normalize+log` in `shared/lib/errorHandling`, move toast behavior to `shared/components` or feature-level handlers, and make UI display an opt-in callback policy that callers wire explicitly.

findings.summary: Supabase client contract text conflicts with actual import-time behavior.
evidence: Comment block says no import-time side effects, but line 55 exports `supabase` from immediate function execution.
That function can install instrumentation and initialize runtime before call sites explicitly request a client.
No top-level contract section in README describes this behavior for subsystem consumers.
findings.suggestion: Make exported API honest: either (a) export a getter (`getSupabaseClient`) and stop eager module-scope creation, or (b) keep eager export but rewrite comments/docs to explicitly declare import-time initialization contract and allowed call sites.

findings.summary: Large flat utility package obscures ownership; one generic utils file became a cross-domain hub.
evidence: `src/shared/lib/` has 68 files and mixed domains; `utils.ts` is a 142-importer hub containing unrelated UI/text/time helpers.
`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` and `src/types/shots.ts` are high fan-in compatibility chokepoints in separate packages, reinforcing hub-and-spoke imports into generic shared areas.
Current placement encourages convenience imports over domain ownership, increasing coupling and migration friction.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 target folders: create `src/shared/lib/ui/cn.ts`, `src/shared/lib/text/truncate.ts`, `src/shared/lib/time/formatTime.ts`, and `src/shared/contracts/types/` for compatibility barrels. Stage 2 move order: first extract and re-export from `utils.ts`, then migrate call sites for `cn`, then text/time helpers, then relocate `shots.ts` barrel to contracts namespace. Stage 3 remove legacy pass-throughs after migration window. Import-update/validation commands: `desloppify move src/shared/lib/utils.ts src/shared/lib/utils/index.ts --dry-run`, `rg -l "@/shared/lib/utils" src`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test -- src/shared/components/ui/button.test.tsx src/integrations/supabase/bootstrap/createSupabaseClient.test.ts`.

findings.summary: Timeline rules are split across UI algorithms, write queues, and evolving SQL functions.
findings.evidence: Frontend enforces frame spacing/quantization and drag constraints while persistence code also normalizes/reorders through queued writes and RPC paths; migration history shows repeated corrective rewrites of the same timeline core behavior, indicating unstable ownership of timeline invariants.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical timeline domain contract (ordering, spacing, normalization rules) in a shared core module and make both UI helpers and DB write paths consume that contract; reduce DB migrations to schema concerns and keep rule logic versioned in one place.

findings.summary: Add-to-shot behavior is duplicated with different retries, optimism, and error handling semantics.
findings.evidence: All three modules perform the same core operations (select target shot, mutate add/add-without-position, optimistic update and rollback, toast/error handling) but with different policies (retry only in gallery, different toast systems, different optimistic key strategies).
findings.suggestion: Extract a single shot-mutation domain hook/service with policy injection (retry, optimistic strategy, user feedback adapter) and keep UI hooks as thin adapters.

findings.summary: Lightbox orchestration uses many grouped interfaces but still centralizes too many concerns.
findings.evidence: The shared-state and image-edit orchestrator hooks aggregate large concern sets (variants, navigation, joins, lineage, shot creation, layout, button props, multiple edit modes), while `MediaLightbox` still performs extensive prop grouping and routing decisions.
findings.suggestion: Move from broad orchestrator hooks to explicit feature slices (e.g., variants slice, shot-actions slice, edit-modes slice) with typed facades per lightbox mode so ownership and change impact are localized.

findings.summary: Project-level cache hooks duplicate lifecycle/query glue with minor behavior differences.
findings.evidence: Both hooks repeat the same `ProjectScopedCache` wiring, ref mirroring for query data, cache update effects, cache read fallbacks, clear/delete helpers, and invalidate/refetch plumbing.
findings.suggestion: Create a generic `useProjectScopedCacheQuery` foundation hook (with pluggable fetcher/key/selectors/invalidation hooks) and reimplement both cache hooks as typed specializations.

findings.title: UI and hook style islands create convention drift across sibling modules
findings.evidence: Assigned UI primitives use a distinct quote/semicolon style and declaration pattern (`src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx:1`, `src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx:1`) while adjacent shared modules and many assigned hooks use different conventions; `src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx` also mixes both styles internally.
findings.suggestion: Standardize formatting and hook/style conventions in shared UI and hook modules using a codemod + lint autofix pass, then enforce via ESLint/Prettier rules in CI to keep siblings aligned.

findings.title: Error reporting strategy is fragmented between centralized handler and ad-hoc console/silent catches
findings.evidence: `handleError` is intended as shared strategy (`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts:24`), but assigned files still mix `console.error` diagnostics and catch-swallow blocks (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts:57`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts:507`, `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/StrokeOverlay.tsx:166`).

findings.title: Authorization boundary handling differs across adjacent Supabase-facing modules
findings.evidence: One edge function enforces service-role request auth (`supabase/functions/calculate-task-cost/index.ts:186-196`), while assigned `complete_task` modules rely on injected privileged clients/keys without in-file verification (`supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts:34-41`), and browser client is also exposed globally (`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts:9-10`).

findings.summary: Related data hooks expose incompatible error contracts (throw/null/UI-state), increasing caller ambiguity.
evidence: `useProjectCRUD` mixes `throw`, `return null`, and toast/reporting side effects inside core CRUD methods.
`useSharePageData` converts RPC errors to local `error` string state and exits without throwing on some failure paths.
`generationTaskBridge` throws lookup errors while wrappers separately log via `handleError`, creating split responsibility.
findings.suggestion: Define one cross-app async contract for data operations (for example `Result<T, AppError>`), apply it first to project/share/task-bridge services, and keep UI translation (toasts/messages) at the component edge instead of in low-level CRUD helpers.

findings.summary: Large orchestration modules centralize too many concerns and incur high indirection cost.
evidence: `ShotImageManagerContainer` builds nested manager state from many hooks and coordinates drag/drop, selection, lightbox, segments, and mobile gestures in one container.
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel` forwards broad state/action sets into panel rendering with a very large dependency array and multiple mode branches.
Holistic abstraction signals mark both areas as indirection hotspots with high chain counts.
findings.suggestion: Split each orchestrator by stable subdomain boundaries (navigation, edit state, media lineage, panel rendering), expose narrow typed facades per boundary, and keep top-level composition limited to wiring those facades together.

findings.summary: Legacy metadata/schema compatibility is still embedded in runtime hot paths.
evidence: `segmentSettingsMigration` reads/writes multiple generations of schema (`pair_*`, `user_overrides`, `additional_loras`, `segmentOverrides`) rather than a single canonical shape.
`useStructureVideo` explicitly supports legacy single-video plus new array-based structure-video formats and additional old key variants.
Legacy support branches are part of normal execution, not isolated one-time migration tooling.
findings.suggestion: Run a staged migration to one canonical schema: (1) add write-only canonical output with telemetry for legacy reads, (2) backfill historical rows server-side, (3) remove legacy-read branches after telemetry reaches near-zero and delete `segmentSettingsMigrationLegacy` references.

findings.summary: Client auth gating uses parallel strategies and retains an unused guard hook.
evidence: Routing performs a synchronous localStorage-based redirect check (`hasStoredSession`) before provider auth state is authoritative.
`Layout` separately gates by `useAuth()` and redirects unauthenticated users.
`useAuthGuard.ts` has no non-test imports, indicating an unfinished migration from earlier auth guard logic.
findings.suggestion: Converge to a single auth gate pipeline: remove `useAuthGuard`, keep provider-driven auth truth, and convert route-loader prechecks to lightweight hints only (never authoritative redirects) or remove them entirely if startup latency remains acceptable.

findings.summary: Large shared directories accumulate mixed concerns and high cross-module coupling.
evidence: Structure metrics show `src/shared/hooks/` (97 files) and `src/shared/components/` (80 files) with broad dependency pull across domains.
Coupling matrix edges are high for shared-to-shared and shared-to-integration links, indicating shared folders acting as feature hosts.
Tool-specific concerns (travel/media/lightbox orchestration) are split between `shared/` and `tools/` instead of cohesive feature packages.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) Create target folders `src/features/media-lightbox/`, `src/features/shot-image-manager/`, `src/features/projects/`, and keep `src/shared/ui/` for pure reusable primitives only. 2) Move in order: `MediaLightbox/*` -> `ShotImageManager/*` -> project CRUD/settings services; after each stage run import updates (`desloppify move <src> <dst> --dry-run`, then execute without `--dry-run`) and search for stale imports (`rg -n "@/shared/components/MediaLightbox|@/shared/components/ShotImageManager|@/shared/hooks/useProjectCRUD" src`). 3) Validate each stage with `npm run typecheck` and focused tests (for example `npm run test -- src/shared/components/MediaLightbox src/shared/components/ShotImageManager src/shared/hooks/useProjectCRUD`) before proceeding.
